summary: Successfully tested the 'Soft skills à développer' feature. Backend returns a structured list in job_narrative.soft_skills_to_develop with nom, importance, and description fields. Frontend displays skills with 'Prioritaire' (critique) and 'Recommandé' (importante) badges correctly.

Backend Test 1 - soft_skills_to_develop.details: POST /api/job-match returns job_narrative with soft_skills_to_develop array (up to 4 items)

Backend Test 4 - Max count limit.details: soft_skills_to_develop is limited to 4 skills maximum (code line 2323)

Backend Test 5 - Different job queries.details: Different jobs (développeur web, infirmier, commercial, data scientist) return appropriate skills

Backend Test 6 - job_narrative structure.details: job_narrative contains both soft_skills_to_develop list and axes_progression text

Frontend Test - UI Display.details: Section shows skill cards with badges (⚡ PRIORITAIRE, 📈 RECOMMANDÉ), skill names, descriptions, and development tip

critical_code_review_comments: ✓ Backend function generate_fallback_job_narrative (lines 2290-2360) correctly calculates soft_skills_to_develop
✓ Skills are filtered based on user competences to show only skills user needs to develop
✓ Frontend JobNarrative component (lines 2760-2800) displays structured list with badges
✓ CSS styles (.soft-skills-to-develop-list, .skill-to-develop-item) provide good visual styling
context_for_next_testing_agent: The 'Soft skills à développer' feature is fully functional. Test file test_soft_skills_to_develop.py covers all requirements. Backend returns up to 4 skills from job requirements that user doesn't already have. Each skill has nom, description, importance (critique/importante). Frontend displays cards with Prioritaire (yellow) or Recommandé (green) badges.
